matter.title: Riverbend Analytics LLC v. John G. Inglewood (residential foreclosure)
matter.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC seeks to foreclose the FHA-insured 30-year fixed mortgage on John G. Inglewood's home at 31 Birchwood Terrace, Biddeford; the borrowers, about 13 months delinquent after an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter, contest the § 6111 notice and two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note, while pursuing an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien in the Foreclosure Diversion Program.

fact_pattern.summary: Riverbend Analytics LLC seeks to foreclose the FHA-insured 30-year fixed mortgage on John G. Inglewood's home at 31 Birchwood Terrace, Biddeford; the borrowers, about 13 months delinquent after an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter, contest the § 6111 notice and two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note, while pursuing an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien in the Foreclosure Diversion Program.
fact_pattern.narrative:
John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley bought their home at 31 Birchwood Terrace, Biddeford, Sagadahoc County with a FHA-insured 30-year fixed loan of $156000 at 6.500%, originated 2020-02-27, payment about $1272 monthly. After an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter, they missed the 2025-09-30 payment and are now roughly 13 months behind, owing about $267500 in principal with an escrow shortage near $5512.

The servicer issued a notice of right to cure dated 2025-12-09 stating an arrearage of $24756 and a cure deadline of 2026-01-13 — thirty-five days, as 14 M.R.S. § 6111 requires. The borrowers could not raise the $22848 reinstatement figure, and Riverbend Analytics LLC filed its foreclosure complaint on 2026-02-03 in Bath District Court. Complicating the ledger, two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note; also, the loan was service-transferred mid-default and the payment history arrived incomplete.

The case entered the Foreclosure Diversion Program. At the first mediation on 2026-04-16, the borrowers submitted a complete loss-mitigation package showing current household income of about $3435 per month; the servicer's review produced an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien, to be finalized at the 2026-06-14 session. Counsel Felix K. Loe of Greenhill Law Offices has answered, preserving defenses that the § 6111 notice misstates the amount due and that the plaintiff must prove ownership of both note and mortgage.

The home is worth roughly $270000, so equity — and Maine's post-Greenleaf standing doctrine — give the borrowers real leverage: if mediation fails, the lender must prove the entire chain of the note's negotiation at trial.
undisputed_facts: The borrowers signed the note and mortgage on 2020-02-27 for $156000.
No payment has been made since 2025-09-30.
A notice of right to cure issued 2025-12-09 with a deadline of 2026-01-13.
disputed_facts: Whether the § 6111 notice states the amount due accurately given two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note.
Whether the plaintiff owns both the note and the mortgage and can prove the chain of transfers.
The correct arrearage and reinstatement figures.

timeline.event: Note and mortgage signed: $156000 at 6.500%, FHA-insured 30-year fixed.
First missed payment after an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter.
Notice of right to cure issued under 14 M.R.S. § 6111 stating $24756 due.
Foreclosure complaint filed; borrowers request Foreclosure Diversion Program mediation.
First FDP mediation: complete loss-mitigation package submitted.
Second FDP mediation scheduled to finalize an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien.

third_parties.name: Summit Logistics Co.
third_parties.relevance: Ran the foreclosure title search and identified parties in interest.

third_parties.name: Omar C. Roe
third_parties.relevance: Prepared the borrower's loss-mitigation package and budget.

third_parties.name: Maine Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection
third_parties.relevance: Received the lender's § 6111 notice filing and the borrower's servicing complaint.

third_parties.name: Maria S. Ashford
third_parties.relevance: Conducts the court-supervised mediation sessions under M.R. Civ. P. 93.

third_parties.name: Northstar Partners Corp.
third_parties.relevance: Services the note for the plaintiff; source of the payment history and the § 6111 notice.

exchanges.question: What happened that you fell behind?
exchanges.answer: an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter. We kept paying what we could until 2025-09-30, then it just wasn't possible.

exchanges.question: What did the cure notice say?
exchanges.answer: It came dated 2025-12-09 and said we owed $24756 by 2026-01-13. We think that number is wrong because of two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note.

exchanges.question: Tell me about the payment dispute.
exchanges.answer: There's two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note. We've asked the servicer for a full history and never gotten a straight answer — and the loan was service-transferred mid-default and the payment history arrived incomplete.

exchanges.question: What can you afford now?
exchanges.answer: About $3435 a month comes in. The counselor says that supports a modified payment if they capitalize the arrears.

exchanges.question: What outcome do you want?
exchanges.answer: To keep the house. We want the modification — an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien — not a sale on the courthouse steps.

exchanges.question: Where are we in the process?
exchanges.answer: First session was 2026-04-16; the second is set for 2026-06-14 to finalize the offer.

goals: Keep the home through a sustainable loan modification finalized in FDP mediation.
Force an accurate accounting resolving two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note.
Preserve § 6111 and standing defenses as leverage if mediation fails.
priorities: The modification — an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien — on terms within the household budget.
No waiver of defenses while mediation proceeds.
desired_outcomes: A permanent modification and dismissal of the foreclosure.
non_starters: A modification with a payment above what $3435 monthly income supports.

constraints.budget: Limited; representation is partly through the volunteer lawyers project.
constraints.timeline: Mediation sessions 2026-04-16 and 2026-06-14 control the near term.

issues.issue: Plaintiff's ownership of the note and mortgage (standing)
issues.governing_law: Bank of America, N.A. v. Greenleaf, 2014 ME 89; 14 M.R.S. § 6321
sub_questions: Can the plaintiff prove the chain of the note's negotiation?
Does a MERS-sourced assignment convey the mortgage?

issues.issue: Sufficiency of the notice of right to cure
sub_questions: Does the notice state the amount due accurately?
Is a defective notice fatal to the action?
issues.client_position: The stated arrearage is wrong because of two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note.

sub_questions: Does $3435 monthly income support the modified payment?

evidence.title: Complete payment history
evidence.description: Servicer life-of-loan history showing two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note.

evidence.title: FDP mediator's report
evidence.description: Report of the 2026-04-16 session and the pending an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien.

evidence.title: Assignment chain and note endorsements
evidence.description: Recorded assignments and the note's allonges — the Greenleaf proof.

causes_of_action.against: John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley
elements: Existence of the mortgage and breach of its condition
Ownership of the note and mortgage
Compliance with 14 M.R.S. § 6111 notice of right to cure
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Judgment of foreclosure and sale; the statutory 90-day redemption period

elements: Holder status or entitlement to enforce

affirmative_defenses.defense: Unclean hands / breach of the duty of good faith
affirmative_defenses.basis: The servicer dual-tracked: it advanced foreclosure while the complete loss-mitigation package was under review.
Payments held in suspense were never applied to the note.

affirmative_defenses.defense: Failure to comply with FHA pre-foreclosure servicing requirements
affirmative_defenses.basis: For an FHA loan, 24 C.F.R. § 203.604 face-to-face requirements are a condition precedent.

discovery.description: Income and hardship documentation supporting loss mitigation.
Rule 30(b)(6)-style deposition on the boarding of records after the loan was service-transferred mid-default and the payment history arrived incomplete.
Chain of note transfers, suspense-account postings, and force-placed insurance.

motions.title: Motion to dismiss for non-compliance with 14 M.R.S. § 6111
motions.relief: Dismissal without prejudice for a defective cure notice.

docket.entry: Complaint for foreclosure filed with certificate of § 6111 compliance.
Borrowers' answer, affirmative defenses, and request for FDP mediation.
Case referred to the Foreclosure Diversion Program; scheduling order issued.
First mediation session held; mediator's report notes complete package under review.

communications.to: John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley
communications.subject: Notice of default and right to cure — 31 Birchwood Terrace
communications.summary: The statutory § 6111 cure notice stating the arrearage and 35-day deadline.
communications.body: Your FHA-insured 30-year fixed loan is in default; the last payment received was applied to the period before 2025-09-30. To cure, pay $24756 on or before 2026-01-13. If you do not cure, we may accelerate the debt and commence foreclosure. You may be eligible for housing counseling assistance; a list of approved counselors is enclosed as required by 14 M.R.S. § 6111.

communications.subject: Hardship letter and request for loss-mitigation review
communications.summary: Borrowers explain the hardship and ask for modification review before the deadline.
communications.body: We fell behind because of an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter. Our household now brings in about $3435 per month and we can sustain a modified payment. We dispute the amount in your notice — two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note — and we ask that you review us for all loss-mitigation options before taking further action.

communications.subject: Notice of representation; error resolution request — 31 Birchwood Terrace
communications.summary: Defense counsel appears, disputes the ledger under RESPA, and preserves § 6111 objections.
communications.body: This office represents John G. Inglewood and John D. Kingsley. Treat this as a notice of error under 12 C.F.R. § 1024.35: the account reflects two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note, and the § 6111 notice's stated arrearage of $24756 appears inaccurate. Provide the life-of-loan history and the identity of the note's owner. Any foreclosure filed on a defective notice will be met with a motion to dismiss.

communications.subject: FDP mediation scheduled — session 1
communications.summary: The court's diversion program schedules the first mediation and lists required documents.
communications.body: The first mediation session is scheduled for 2026-04-16. The lender shall appear with authority to modify and a current reinstatement figure; the borrowers shall bring a complete loss-mitigation application, proof of income, and a budget prepared with their housing counselor, per M.R. Civ. P. 93.

communications.subject: Loss-mitigation decision — 31 Birchwood Terrace
communications.summary: The servicer's underwriting produces a modification offer to be finalized at session 2.
communications.body: Underwriting has completed review of the package submitted at mediation. Based on documented income of $3435 per month, the investor approves an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien. Draft documents will issue before the 2026-06-14 session; the trial-period payment schedule is attached. The pending motion practice is unaffected until final documents are executed.

communications.subject: The modification offer — what it means
communications.summary: Counsel explains the offer, the arrears treatment, and the strategy at session 2.
communications.body: The servicer has offered an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien. On your income of about $3435 a month this is workable, and it resolves the arrearage without a lump sum. We will not dismiss our § 6111 and standing defenses until the permanent modification is signed — if the trial payments go through, the foreclosure is dismissed; if the deal falls apart, we litigate the defective notice and the note's chain of ownership.

facts.hardship: an unreimbursed furnace and roof failure the same winter
facts.payment_dispute: two payments the servicer applied to a suspense account instead of the note
facts.servicing_transfer: the loan was service-transferred mid-default and the payment history arrived incomplete
facts.modification_offer: an FHA-HAMP partial claim deferring the arrears to a junior lien
